channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Progressive powerhouses launch primary war against Democratic establishment ahead of 2026 elections
item.description: Durham County Commissioner Nida Allam launched a Democratic primary challenge against Rep. Valerie Foushee, D-N.C., with endorsements from Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., and progressive groups.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department faces hurdle in seeking case against Comey as judge finds constitutional problems
item.description: A federal judge says the Justice Department violated the constitutional rights of a close friend of James Comey and must return to him computer files that prosecutors had hoped to use for a potential criminal case against the former FBI director.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate rejects extension of health care subsidies
item.description: The Senate has rejected legislation to extend Affordable Care Act tax credits, essentially guaranteeing that millions of Americans will see a steep rise in costs at the beginning of the year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Jeanne Shaheen warns millions could lose coverage as ACA votes near defeat
item.description: With ACA tax credits set to expire, Sen. Jeanne Shaheen says premiums are "doubling, tripling" and warns "millions" could lose coverage if Congress fails to act.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep, MPR News

item.title: Redistricting in Indiana was supposed to be a slam dunk. It has proven anything but
item.description: Republican-led states have raced to redraw congressional lines to advantage their own party. But the effort has hit unexpected pushback in Indiana, and become a test of Trump's grip on his party.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas, MPR News

item.title: Nisswa Mayor Jennifer Carnahan faces criticism
item.description: The city’s four council members spoke critically of Carnahan in the wake of allegations that a Nisswa restaurant employee overheard her disparaging a city council member. Carnahan later went on to allege that the woman assaulted her.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Will U.S. military strikes slow drug overdose deaths? Experts say no
item.description: President Trump says U.S. strikes on supposed drug-smuggling boats will save Americans from overdose deaths. But most experts worry the strategy is counterproductive.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: Trump once denied using this slur about Haiti and African nations. Now he boasts about it
item.description: President Donald Trump admits he used the slur “s---hole countries” during a private 2018 meeting with lawmakers to disparage Haiti and African nations. On Tuesday, he recalled the comment at a rally in Pennsylvania, despite previously denying it.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minneapolis council member pushes for more public restrooms downtown
item.description: Katie Cashman, who represents Ward 7, joined Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition Tuesday to talk about the pros, cons and concerns of her bathroom proposal for the Nicollet Avenue transit corridor.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Federal budget cuts blamed for MN property tax hikes
item.description: The Blue Earth County Board votes next week on a proposed property tax increase that officials say is needed to pay for the rising costs of administering SNAP, Medicaid and other federal programs that federal legislation shifts onto local governments.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Duluth City Council votes to transfer former golf course land for future housing
item.description: Despite concerns voiced by some residents claiming a lack of transparency and a flawed public process, the Duluth City Council voted to transfer the closed Lester Park Golf Course property to the city’s economic development authority to explore a potential housing development.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News
